Men's Bottomwear  ·  Category 01

Denim

Fit distribution, colour depth and price positioning — three brands, head to head.

14

Relaxed fit — Azorte's strongest commercial denim call

14 relaxed options in Blue, Black, Grey, Off White and Washed Blue at Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,300. Highest visibility and the right fit to anchor the denim fixture. H&M leads overall at 61 options by going deep into loose straight and baggy — both of which Azorte is underweight in.

Options by Fit — All Brands
Denim Option Distribution
Price Architecture
Price Range Positioning
Azorte
Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,300
H&M
Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,999
Westside
Rs 1,499 to Rs 2,499
Rs 1,499Rs 1,999Rs 2,500Rs 3,000
Key Observations
Strength
Relaxed fit — 14 options, strongest commercial anchor
Blue, Black, Grey, Off White and Washed Blue at Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,300. Azorte's highest visibility denim fit and the right bet commercially. Strong colour depth makes this the best presented denim category in the store.
Strength
Baggy and Loose — Azorte is in the game, unlike before
4 baggy options and 7 loose options — Azorte has entered both trend fits. The direction is right. The depth needs to grow: H&M has 12 baggy and 16 loose to Azorte's 4 and 7 respectively.
Gap
Barrel fit entirely absent — Westside has 9 options
Westside stocks 9 barrel options at Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,499 in Blue and Grey. A fashion-forward silhouette with strong visual appeal on fixture. Azorte has zero presence here.
Gap
Straight fit under-indexed at only 5 options
Straight is a core commercial denim fit. H&M has 18 options across original and regular straight, Westside has 9. Azorte has just 5 in Black, Dark Blue and Grey — too thin for a category staple.
Opportunity
Add barrel and deepen straight to compete on core and trend
3 to 4 barrel options at Rs 2,299 and 4 to 5 more straight options in Blue and Navy would meaningfully close the gap on both the commercial and fashion denim fronts without a large investment.
Fabric Note
Azorte denim is the most durable of the three brands
Slightly heavier initially but sturdy and gets comfortable with wear. H&M is softest at first touch. Westside sits in between. H&M's loose and baggy fits have the best visual fall on body.

Baggy is 37% of H&M's entire denim assortment at Rs 2,700. Neither Azorte nor Westside stocks this fit. Fastest-growing silhouette with shoppers under 25.

Azorte leads cargos on total options — but Westside wins on variety and value

Azorte has 27 cargo options vs Westside's 26 and H&M's 22. Azorte leads on straight and relaxed depth. Westside wins on fashion variety with 14 pocketed cargo options at Rs 1,699 — well below Azorte's Rs 2,300 entry.

Options by Fit
Cargo Option Distribution
Price Range
Cargo Price Range by Brand
Azorte
Rs 2,300 to Rs 2,500
H&M
Rs 2,699 to Rs 2,999
Westside
Rs 1,699 to Rs 2,499
Rs 1,699Rs 2,300Rs 2,699Rs 3,000
Key Observations
Strength
Straight cargo — 12 options, best value-for-money in the range
Olive, Beige, Off White and Light Green at Rs 2,300 to Rs 2,500. Deepest straight cargo assortment across all three brands and good colour spread. Pocket construction is functional and holds up well on body.
Strength
4-Way Stretch — 8 options, best comfort perception of all brands
Olive, Khaki, Grey and Navy at Rs 2,500. The most comfortable cargo across all three brands on body. Excellent movement and wearability. An underexposed product that deserves front fixture positioning.
Gap
No pocketed fashion cargo — Westside has 14 options at Rs 1,699
Westside's pocketed fashion cargo in Olive, Stone and Beige at Rs 1,699 to Rs 2,299 is their strongest visual cargo. Azorte has nothing with this styling. The look draws younger shoppers and Azorte misses the category entirely.
Gap
No loose fit cargo — H&M has 14 options at Rs 2,699
H&M's loose cargo in Black, Navy and Olive is a strong direction. Azorte has no loose cargo at all. A growing demand signal with 18 to 28 year old shoppers that Azorte currently cannot serve.
Opportunity
Utility jogger — Westside has 8 options, Azorte has zero
Westside's utility jogger in Black and Olive at Rs 1,699 to Rs 1,999 is a comfortable everyday category. Azorte is entirely absent. A lower-risk entry point that also lowers the overall cargo entry price from Rs 2,300.
Fabric Note
Straight and Relaxed cargo fabric at Azorte holds up well — functional pocket construction is a genuine strength
Pocket loops and straps hold their shape on body. H&M cargo pockets are more decorative than functional. Westside relaxed utility is the most comfortable overall but Azorte straight cargo sits cleaner for an office-adjacent look.

Gap — Azorte
Slim cargo is H&M's second biggest bet
H&M puts 25% of cargo into slim fit at Rs 1,999 — most accessible in their range. Five colours, strong commercial selection. Azorte has nothing here.
Gap — Azorte
Loose cargo — H&M's biggest fit by volume
31% of H&M's entire cargo assortment is loose fit at Rs 2,699. Navy, Black and Olive covered. Azorte is not present at all.
Gap — Azorte
Jogger cargo — Westside's everyday play
Westside stocks three jogger cargo styles at Rs 1,699 to Rs 1,899 in Olive, Black and Stone. Comfortable, functional and everyday. Azorte misses this segment completely.

Azorte leads H&M on trouser depth — but Westside's 83 options show the gap in formal and fashion categories

Azorte has 75 trouser options — ahead of H&M's 56. The strength is in comfort and stretch categories. Westside leads at 83 with 18 formal trouser options and 14 pocketed fashion cargos that Azorte has nothing comparable to.

Key Observations
Strength
Widest price range — Rs 1,499 to Rs 3,300, most options in the Rs 2,500 band
Azorte has the widest trouser price architecture of all three brands. The Rs 2,500 band — 4-Way Stretch, Formal Stretch and Satin Stretch — is a well-stocked mid-premium comfort cluster that neither H&M nor Westside has an equivalent to.
Strength
Satin Stretch — 6 options, best handfeel in the entire range
Grey, Beige and Navy at Rs 2,500. Premium handfeel and excellent drape. The best performing trouser on body across all three brands. Currently underexposed on fixture — should be pulled to front.
Gap
No formal trouser — Westside has 18 options at Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,999
Westside's formal trouser in Brown, Navy and Beige is their single biggest trouser category. Azorte has Formal Stretch but it reads as utility — not as a true formal trouser. A consistent footfall driver that Azorte is not fully serving.
Gap
No linen blend — H&M has 6 options, Westside has 10
H&M at Rs 2,700 in White, Brown, Blue and Black. Westside at Rs 2,299 to Rs 2,999 in White and Beige. A premium summer fabric category that both competitors cover and Azorte is entirely absent from.
Fabric Note
Pro Earth and Satin Stretch have the best handfeel in the range — both underexposed on fixture
Satin Stretch drapes cleanly and feels premium at first touch. Pro Earth in Olive, Ecru and Brown at Rs 2,500 to Rs 3,300 is a sustainable range with no competitor equivalent. Both products sit too far back on the current fixture layout.

Pro Earth — Azorte's premium differentiator

52 Options at Rs 2,500 to Rs 3,300. Neither H&M nor Westside has a sustainability narrative in trousers. This is the one category where Azorte is not catching up — it is ahead.

Men's Tops  ·  Category 04

Polo & Shirts

Four polo tiers, organic cotton quality, and a shirt wall that needs more visual variety.

Azorte's polo range is competitive — but shirts at 10 options lose to H&M's 20 and Westside's 18

Azorte has 25 polo options — ahead of H&M's 23 and close to Westside's 29. Entry price parity across all three brands (Rs 799 to Rs 999). The shirt gap is the real issue: Azorte has only cotton solids while H&M has 20 options across two shirt types and Westside has 18 commercial cotton shirts.

Price Range
Polo Price Range — All Brands
Azorte
Rs 800 to Rs 1,899
H&M
Rs 799 to Rs 1,499
Westside
Rs 999 to Rs 1,999
Rs 799Rs 999Rs 1,499Rs 1,899
Shirt Price Range — All Brands
Azorte
Rs 999 to Rs 1,499
H&M
Rs 999 to Rs 1,999
Westside
Rs 999 to Rs 1,699
Rs 999Rs 1,299Rs 1,699Rs 1,999
Key Observations
Strength
Casual polo — 12 options, widest colour variety of all three brands
White, Yellow, Pink, Olive and Light Blue at Rs 800 to Rs 1,299. Azorte experiments with colour where H&M and Westside are more conservative. The best colour variety in the polo category across all brands.
Strength
Patterned polo — 8 options, unique positioning in this price band
Multi-pattern prints at Rs 1,299 to Rs 1,499. A partywear-casual positioning that neither H&M nor Westside replicates. A genuine differentiator in the Rs 1,299 to Rs 1,499 range.
Gap
Shirt wall has only 10 options — all solid, no prints, no formal
Azorte has 10 cotton shirt options in solids only. H&M has 20 across cotton and basic shirts. Westside has 18 commercial cotton shirts with better colour depth. One printed or formal range would close this gap immediately.
Quality Risk
Casual polo collar curls after washing
The Rs 800 entry polo has a collar construction issue — it folds after wash. This is a return and complaint trigger. Position Pro Earth and Patterned tiers at the front of the polo fixture instead.
Opportunity
Printed shirts — entirely absent at Azorte, Westside has strong visual impact
Westside's printed shirt range creates strong visual movement on the fixture and draws attention across the store. A small printed range at Rs 1,299 to Rs 1,599 at Azorte would completely transform the shirt wall presence.
Fabric Note
Pro Earth polo has the best on-body quality — collar stays flat, most breathable of the three brands
Olive, Off White and Mustard at Rs 1,699 to Rs 1,899. Softer and more breathable than H&M and Westside equivalents. Collar stays flat unlike the Casual polo which curls after wash. Should lead the polo fixture.
Azorte Polo Tiers
Casual
Rs 800
Entry tier. Good colour variety. Collar curls after wash — quality risk. Should not anchor the fixture.
Patterned
Rs 999 to Rs 1,200
Subtle texture prints. Adds visual variety. Currently underexposed on the floor — needs more space.
Pro Earth
Rs 1,200 to Rs 1,500
Heavier than Casual, holds shape better. Sustainability narrative. Mid-tier positioning with good movement.
Organic Cotton ★
Rs 1,899
Best product in the range. Collar stays flat, softest feel, most breathable. Should anchor the polo fixture — currently not shown up front.
Best Product
Organic Cotton at Rs 1,899 — anchor the fixture
Noticeably softer and more breathable. Collar stays flat and drapes cleanly. The quality difference is perceptible at first touch. This is the product Azorte should lead with on the polo wall.
Quality Risk
Casual Polo collar curls after washing
Collar folding is a return and complaint trigger. Do not let this be the first product a customer picks up. Position it behind the Organic and Pro Earth tiers on the fixture.
Opportunity
Patterned Polo needs more floor space
Patterned polos exist at Azorte but are buried. Westside has 15 to 25 Options of textured knit polos prominently displayed. This tier deserves more fixture real estate.

Cotton Solid

Formal — White, Sky Blue, Navy, Grey
Strongest shirt wall — multi-zone visual Printed shirts draw attention across the store Rs 999 to Rs 2,499
Gap
Azorte shirt wall is monotone — all solids, no print break
The fixture reads flat. Good cotton quality underneath but the visual impression at point of wear is weak. H&M and Westside both have printed options that create movement on the wall. One printed shirt range would transform the fixture completely.
Gap
No formal shirts at Azorte
Westside stocks crisp formal shirts in White, Sky Blue, Navy and Grey at Rs 999 to Rs 2,499. A steady category for office-casual and occasion buyers. Entirely absent at Azorte.
Opportunity
Printed shirts would transform the shirt fixture
Westside printed shirts (florals, abstract) draw attention from across the store. A small printed range at Rs 1,299 to Rs 1,799 would break the solid monotony and signal more fashion intent without a large investment.
On Body — Quality and Fit Notes
On Body — Azorte Polo
Organic Cotton is the most comfortable polo of all three brands
Softest at first touch, collar stays flat, excellent breathability. Pro Earth sits in the middle — heavier than Casual, holds shape better. Casual polo is decent but the collar curling after wash is a real risk.
On Body — H&M
Shirts look cleaner and sharper while wearing
Better visual finish and silhouette while standing. Collar quality is consistent across the range. H&M wins on how things look at point of wear — even when the fabric is not actually better underneath.
On Body — Westside
Formal shirts are the standout — crisp and structured
Formal shirts in White, Sky Blue, Navy and Grey are crisp and well-structured. Printed shirts are the visual standout — they draw attention from the fixture. Cotton quality at the base level is comparable to Azorte.
Azorte Cotton Shirt
Good product, poor fixture presentation
Good cotton quality — breathable, clean hang, tight seams. But the solid-only range reads flat and does not attract the eye. The product is better than the fixture presentation suggests. A print range would fix this completely.

Entry Price Story
AZORTE
₹699
Basic printed kurta
1.14x
price gap
WESTSIDE
₹799
Basic ethnic top
The real story: Both brands enter ethnic at almost the same price level — Rs 699 vs Rs 799 is a negligible gap. The meaningful difference is not entry price but what sits above it. Westside has a full premium embroidered and festive range from Rs 1,499 to Rs 5,999. Azorte stops at Rs 3,799 and has no festive product at all.
Category Depth — Options by Sub-Category
Options — Azorte vs Westside

Kurta pyjama sets
Key Observations
Strength
Pant Kurti Sets — 50 options at a far more accessible price than Westside
Azorte has 50 options at Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,599 in Pink, Beige, Blue, Mint, Yellow and Navy. Westside has 52 but at Rs 2,999 to Rs 3,999 — nearly double the price. Azorte wins this category decisively on value accessibility.
Strength
Basic kurtas at Rs 699 to Rs 999 — lightweight, breathable, everyday-ready
Azorte's basic printed kurtas feel light and easy to wear daily. Good fabric quality at the price point. Westside's base tier starts at Rs 799 in a similar fabric but with a narrower colour range. Azorte has a genuine quality-value advantage at entry level.
Gap
No embroidery anywhere — Westside has 18 premium embroidered options
Westside's premium embroidered kurtas at Rs 1,999 to Rs 3,499 in Ivory, Maroon, Rust and Indigo are a dependable sales category. Azorte has zero embroidery or surface detailing anywhere in the entire ethnic range.
Gap
Men's ethnic has zero festive product — Westside has a complete ecosystem
Azorte has 38 men's ethnic options but none are festive-ready. Westside has 10 kurta pyjama sets at Rs 3,499 to Rs 5,999 and 12 premium embroidered kurtas. A wedding or festival buyer finds nothing at Azorte.
Opportunity
8 to 10 embroidered options would transform the ethnic fixture
Westside's A-line and yoke-embroidered kurtas at Rs 2,599 to Rs 3,999 represent a festive segment entirely absent at Azorte. A small embroidered range at Rs 1,999 to Rs 2,999 would immediately elevate the visual and commercial impact of the ethnic section.
Fabric Note
Premium kurta fabric at Azorte is noticeably smoother than the basic tier — a clear upgrade story on fixture
Premium kurtas at Rs 999 to Rs 1,300 have a heavier hand and smoother finish compared to the basic tier. Westside's premium and embroidered kurtas feel more structured overall. Azorte's fabric story improves significantly as you move up the price ladder.

Part Two  ·  Platform Intelligence  ·  2026

AI Web Scraping
& Retail Intelligence

Four platforms that transform competitor data into commercial decisions — infrastructure, analytics, trend intelligence, and low-code extraction explained step by step for Azorte teams.

Platforms
4
Tools in the AI intelligence stack
Pipeline Stages
10
From raw data to retail decisions
Key Output
Real-Time
Intel
Competitor pricing and assortment
Decision Types
6
Launch, replenish, markdown, exit, price, range
Data
Stack

How the four platforms connect to form a complete intelligence system

Bright Data and Import.io handle data collection and extraction. DataWeave and EDITED handle AI analysis and retail intelligence. Together they cover the full journey from raw competitor URLs to commercial decisions for Azorte's buying, pricing and merchandising teams.

The Four Platforms
BD
Bright Data
Web Scraping Infrastructure
INFRA

The data collection backbone. Solves the core challenge of large-scale web scraping — websites block bots. Bright Data provides IP rotation, anti-bot bypass, and browser automation at scale.

Residential proxy networkCAPTCHA bypassDoes NOT analyse data
DW
DataWeave
AI Retail Analytics
ANALYTICS

All-in-one retail intelligence combining automated ecommerce monitoring with AI analytics. Handles both data collection and analysis — accessible to business teams without engineering support.

AI auto-classificationReal-time alertsNo code needed
ED
EDITED
Fashion Trend Intelligence
TREND

Purpose-built for fashion retail. Goes beyond competitive pricing to deliver strategic trend intelligence, aesthetic tracking, and merchandising optimisation for fashion-forward brands like Azorte.

Trend prediction 4 to 8 weeks aheadAesthetic classificationMarkdown timing
IO
Import.io
Low-Code Data Extraction
EXTRACT

Designed for speed and simplicity. Non-technical teams set up web scrapers using a visual point-and-click interface — ideal for marketing, ecommerce, and content teams who need quick competitive data.

Visual interface — no codeScheduled automationNot enterprise-grade at scale
How the Platforms Connect
1
Targets
URLs, categories, brands to monitor
2
Collect
Bright Data or Import.io extracts raw data
3
Clean
Standardise, deduplicate, normalise attributes
4
Analyse
DataWeave or EDITED runs AI classification
5
Dashboard
Pricing, trend, assortment, markdown reports
6
Decide
Commercial action for Azorte teams
BD
Bright Data
Web Scraping Infrastructure and Proxy Network Layer
INFRASTRUCTURE

Bright Data is not an analytics platform — it is the data collection backbone. It solves the core technical challenge of large-scale web scraping: websites block bots. Bright Data provides the infrastructure to extract data reliably at scale. Think of it as the data plumbing layer that feeds everything else.

Core Components
How It Works
ComponentFunctionWhy It Matters for Azorte
Residential Proxy NetworkRoutes requests through real user IPs worldwidePrevents Zara and H&M from detecting and blocking scrapers
Mobile IP NetworkSimulates mobile device trafficAccurate mobile pricing and experience data
Browser AutomationHeadless browsers simulate human scrolling and clickingCaptures JS-rendered content and dynamic pricing
CAPTCHA BypassAnti-detection and CAPTCHA solving infrastructureScrapes Cloudflare-protected fashion sites reliably
Data Extraction APIsPre-built templates for major ecommerce sitesReduces development time for structured data collection
Step by Step — How Azorte Would Use Bright Data
1
Step 1
Account Setup and Plan Selection
Purchase enterprise scraping plan with proxy access. Select ecommerce scraping APIs appropriate for fashion retailer volume.
2
Step 2
Define Scraping Targets
Specify target URLs: Zara new arrivals, H&M women's tops, Urbanic co-ords. Define scraping frequency — daily for pricing, weekly for assortment.
3
Step 3
Configure the Scraper
Set up data extraction rules: define product name, price, category, and attribute fields. Use Bright Data's pre-built ecommerce templates where available.
4
Step 4
Launch Automated Collection
Activate the scraper. Bright Data automatically rotates IPs, bypasses anti-bot systems, and runs continuous extraction on the defined schedule.
5
Step 5
Receive Structured Output
Data is delivered in CSV, JSON, or directly via API feeds. Raw data can be piped into databases such as Snowflake, PostgreSQL, or cloud storage.
6
Step 6
Feed into Analytics Layer
Send structured data to BI tools (Power BI, Tableau) or to DataWeave and EDITED for AI-powered retail analysis.

Data Flow Summary

IN
Target URLs, data field definitions, scraping schedule
↓
→
IP rotation  →  Anti-bot bypass  →  Browser rendering  →  Data extraction
↓
OUT
Structured CSV, JSON, or API feeds ready for analysis
Important: Bright Data does NOT perform AI analysis. It provides the clean, structured data that AI and analytics platforms then process. Think of it as the data plumbing layer.
DW
DataWeave
AI-Powered Retail Competitive Analytics Platform
ANALYTICS

DataWeave is an all-in-one retail intelligence platform that combines automated ecommerce monitoring with AI-driven analytics. Unlike Bright Data, it handles both data collection and analysis, making it accessible to business teams without engineering support.

Step by Step — How Azorte Would Use DataWeave
1
Step 1
Competitor Onboarding
Select brands, marketplaces, and product categories for monitoring. Configure Azorte's own assortment for direct comparison benchmarking.
2
Step 2
Automated Ecommerce Monitoring
DataWeave continuously tracks pricing, discounts, stock levels, assortment depth, and review ratings across all configured competitors.
3
Step 3
AI Classification Engine
Products are automatically categorised using AI tagging: fit (oversized, slim), aesthetic (resortwear, quiet luxury), fabric, and pricing tier. No manual taxonomy work required.
4
Step 4
Competitive Benchmarking Reports
Access pricing heatmaps, assortment comparison matrices, markdown timing analysis, and category depth reports — all auto-generated on a live dashboard.
5
Step 5
Alerts and Real-Time Insights
Set automated triggers: receive instant alerts when Zara reduces linen shirt pricing, when Urbanic expands a key category, or when Myntra's bestseller mix shifts.
6
Step 6
Dashboard-Driven Decisions
Business teams access role-specific dashboards for daily pricing decisions, merchandising planning, inventory strategy, and launch timing.
Team Usage Map
Who Uses DataWeave and How
TeamDataWeave Use Case
Pricing TeamDaily competitor price tracking, markdown monitoring, price gap analysis
Merchandising TeamCategory saturation analysis, assortment gap identification
Buying TeamAssortment benchmarking, newness tracking, white space detection
Marketing TeamCampaign launch tracking, competitor collection monitoring
Ecommerce TeamBestseller identification, conversion-driving attribute analysis
ED
EDITED
Fashion Merchandising and Trend Intelligence Platform
TREND INTEL

EDITED is purpose-built for fashion retail. It goes beyond competitive pricing to deliver strategic trend intelligence, aesthetic tracking, and merchandising optimisation — essential for fashion-forward brands like Azorte.

Step by Step — How Azorte Would Use EDITED
1
Step 1
Fashion Data Collection Setup
Configure EDITED to track global fashion retailers, luxury brands, and key Indian marketplaces. Define relevant product categories aligned to Azorte's assortment.
2
Step 2
AI Fashion Classification
EDITED's AI automatically classifies all competitor products into detailed fashion attributes: silhouette, aesthetic, colour palette, fit, fabric, and trend tag — coquette, quiet luxury, utility fashion, Y2K.
3
Step 3
Trend Intelligence Tracking
Monitor rising aesthetics before they peak, detect category overcrowding, and identify markdown acceleration patterns. Understand which trends are entering, climbing, or declining.
4
Step 4
Merchandising Recommendations
Receive AI-generated recommendations on when to replenish, when to discount, and when a trend is about to peak — enabling smarter buy decisions before competitors act.
5
Step 5
Visual Analytics and Trend Graphs
Fashion planning teams access visual dashboards showing trend movement curves, colour growth charts, category lifecycle stages, and markdown timing timelines.
6
Step 6
Strategic Planning Application
Apply EDITED insights to future collection planning, influencer collaboration briefs, visual merchandising strategy, and seasonal buying decisions.
Strategic Applications for Azorte
What EDITED Reveals and What Azorte Does With It
ApplicationWhat EDITED RevealsAzorte Action
Merchandising PlanningRising vs declining trends across marketEnter trends early, phase out declining categories
Assortment OptimisationOvercrowded categories and white spacesReduce exposure in oversaturated areas
Markdown OptimisationWhen competitors start discountingTime Azorte markdowns for maximum sell-through
Collection PlanningColour and silhouette direction for next seasonAlign future buys with market trajectory
Competitive PositioningAesthetic territory competitors ownIdentify Azorte's differentiation opportunity
IO
Import.io
Low-Code Web Data Extraction Platform
EXTRACTION

Import.io is designed for speed and simplicity. It enables non-technical teams to set up web scrapers using a visual interface — ideal for marketing, ecommerce, and content teams who need quick competitive data without engineering involvement.

Step by Step — How Azorte Would Use Import.io
1
Step 1
Open Target Website
Navigate to the competitor website to be scraped — Zara.com, Myntra.com, or Urbanic's product catalogue pages.
2
Step 2
Visual Field Selection
Using Import.io's point-and-click interface, click on the data elements to extract: product title, price, discount label, category, and description.
3
Step 3
Pattern Recognition
Import.io automatically detects the webpage structure and identifies recurring product patterns across all similar pages — no code required.
4
Step 4
Automated Extraction
The platform extracts all matching products across paginated category pages automatically, building a complete structured dataset.
5
Step 5
Set Automation Schedule
Configure recurring extraction: hourly for flash sales monitoring, daily for pricing, weekly for assortment breadth tracking.
6
Step 6
Export and Integrate Data
Export as spreadsheets for team use, connect via API to internal dashboards, or pipe into databases for further analysis.
Team Usage Map
Who Uses Import.io and How
TeamImport.io Use Case
Marketing TeamTrack competitor campaign launches, promotional events, new collection drops
Merchandising TeamMonitor category expansion and pricing changes on a weekly basis
Ecommerce TeamIdentify bestseller products and track page placement strategy
Buying TeamQuick audits of competitor newness and product launches
Raw
Data
→ Retail

From web scraping to commercial retail decisions — the full 10-stage pipeline

This is the complete workflow that connects four AI platforms to Azorte's commercial teams. Each stage has a clear purpose, platform, and output. The end result is faster, smarter decisions on pricing, assortment, markdown timing, and category planning.

Stages 4 to 6 — Data Infrastructure
Stage 4
Web Scraping Infrastructure Activation
Deploy the appropriate platform based on team capability and data requirement. Bright Data or Import.io handle extraction. DataWeave and EDITED have built-in collection capabilities.
Stage 5
Proxy and Anti-Bot Systems
Large-scale scraping requires infrastructure to avoid IP blocks and CAPTCHA walls. Handled by Bright Data's proxy network when collecting raw data independently. DataWeave and EDITED manage this internally.
Stage 6
Automated Data Extraction
All platforms support scheduled, automated extraction. Set cadence based on how fast competitor data changes: pricing may need daily pulls, assortment weekly, trend analysis monthly.
Stage 7 — Data Cleaning and Standardisation
Normalising Raw Scraped Data for Analysis

Raw scraped data is inconsistent. Before analysis, teams must standardise naming conventions, remove duplicates, normalise pricing formats, and categorise attributes.

Example: "Relaxed Fit Shirt" and "Oversized Casual Shirt" from different retailers should both map to a single normalised attribute like "oversized-casualwear" for accurate comparison across competitors.
Stage 8 — AI Classification and Analytics
AI Functions Across the Platform Stack
AI FunctionPlatformOutput for Azorte
Trend predictionEDITED, DataWeaveRising aesthetics 4 to 8 weeks ahead of peak
Dynamic pricing analysisDataWeavePrice gap vs competitors by category
Demand forecastingDataWeave, EDITEDCategory-level demand trajectory
Sentiment analysisDataWeaveReview-based product strength indicators
Product matchingDataWeave, Bright DataLike-for-like competitor comparisons
Assortment optimisationEDITED, DataWeaveCategory mix recommendations
Stage 9 — Dashboards and Visualisation
Five Dashboard Types for Azorte Teams
Dashboard TypeKey MetricsPrimary User
Pricing DashboardCompetitor prices, Azorte gap %, markdown timingPricing team
Trend DashboardRising and falling aesthetics, colour growth, silhouette shiftsMerchandising and buying
Assortment DashboardCategory depth comparison, white space mapBuying team
Markdown DashboardDiscount depth, timing, competitor patternsPlanning team
Bestseller DashboardTop-performing Options by competitor, demand rankingEcommerce team
Stage 10 — Retail Decision Execution
🚀
Launch Decision
Enter a rising trend category
Enter a rising trend category before competitors saturate it — timed using EDITED trend trajectory data.
🔁
Replenishment Decision
Build equivalent supply fast
Identify fast-moving competitor Options and build equivalent supply before the demand window closes.
💸
Markdown Decision
Time discounting strategically
Time discounting to align with or get ahead of market clearance cycles — informed by DataWeave competitor markdown data.
🚫
Category Exit Decision
Reduce investment in declining categories
Reduce investment in declining, over-saturated categories before carrying costs escalate.
🎯
Pricing Decision
Adjust prices on competitor gap data
Adjust prices based on competitor gap analysis and positioning goals — updated continuously via DataWeave alerts.
📚
Range Planning Decision
Build next season's buy on data
Build next season's buy informed by trend trajectory data from EDITED — not gut feel or last season's performance alone.
